core_mechanism: A steady-state energy-balance constraint on a spatially distributed emitter population: absorbed power delivered by emitters, integrated over their density profile along the line of sight, is set equal to an absorber's dissipative loss to fix an equilibrium level, and requiring that level not exceed an observed bound converts into an upper limit on the emitters' abundance.
brief_reason: The abstract computes the radiation flux emitted by a source population (enhanced by spin), lets it heat an absorber that cools less or more efficiently, and turns the resulting heating into an upper limit on the source fraction whose stringency depends on the assumed spatial density (halo) profile — i.e. an injection-versus-dissipation equilibrium integrated over a source distribution.
